votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the vowel check, string reversal, consonant counting, and the logic for splitting strings by spaces while ignoring empty segments, matching the Coq specification's behavior.
The Python code correctly implements the vowel check, string reversal, consonant counting, and space-based word splitting logic defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the string splitting, vowel checking, consonant counting, and the specification logic exactly as defined in the Coq code.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions. is_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters. count_consonants counts non-vowel characters identically. split_spaces_nonempty_acc splits on spaces and filters empty strings the same way (the Python iterative version produces the same results as the Coq recursive version). select_words_spec returns true iff ans equals the filtered list of words with exactly n consonants, matching the Coq Prop definition for concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions. is_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters. count_consonants counts non-vowel characters identically. split_spaces_nonempty splits on spaces and filters empty strings the same way (the Python iterative version with acc_rev = ch + acc_rev and rev_string produces the same words as the Coq recursive version). select_words_spec filters words by consonant count and compares to ans, returning a boolean judgment that matches when the Coq Prop would hold. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions. is_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters. count_consonants counts non-vowel characters identically. split_spaces_nonempty_acc splits on spaces and filters empty strings the same way (the iterative Python version produces the same result as the recursive Coq version). select_words_spec returns true iff ans equals the filtered list of words with exactly n consonants, matching the Coq Prop definition for concrete inputs.

Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, string reverse, consonant count (non-vowels), split by spaces into non-empty words preserving order, and filter by count; final comparison matches the Prop equality.

Python mirrors Coq on all concrete behaviors: same vowel set, reverse, consonant count (non-vowel), splitting on ASCII space into non-empty words with correct order and handling of acc_rev, and filtering by equality; select_words_spec returns True exactly when Coq’s equality holds.
Python mirrors Coq: splits on spaces into nonempty words in order, counts consonants as non-vowels, and checks ans equals the filtered list; behaviors match for all concrete inputs in scope.
